item.title: brrrrr
item.content:encoded:
so many fear chills from this.
Awesome article, great atmosphere. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, but I'm really curious about how the D from the 2nd test was found alive after he had the majority of skin and musculature removed. Wouldn't he of bled out or gone into shock from being nearly fully flayed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should probably be Euclid instead of Safe.

Accurate representation of working in retail. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a bit of a difficult issue.

During season 1, a lot of SCP's referenced a "Monthly termination". Enough to warrant a headcanon surrounding a place where D-class are just killed. But that was the old Foundation, and many newer skips don't reference this. I like to think they don't do this anymore, as it is a waste of resources and kind of unnecessary.

There actually is even an SCP about this, the name escapes me though.

EDIT: Found it http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2193

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems cool, but the end was kinda generic, Im probably just being picky though.
Oh and on a picky kinda vibe, it mentions that one of the D-Class were released back to prison, now I might be wrong here, but I don't remember D-Class not being terminated at the end of the month. Might just be misremembering.
No-vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really into this until I reached the final log, which reached clumsy levels of exposition without enough horror to make it palatable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent example of how you don't need to have a bucket of ichor thrown in your face to feel horripilated dread. This shows just enough and tells a little less to great effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking this reminded me a lot of the Series I skip that's like… an abandoned Kmart full of ghosts or something. And as I was pondering whether that was a bad thing or not, I hit this:

Analysis of the extracted liquid showed it to be genetically identical to several hundred past and current residents of Denton, Missouri.

And all righty then!

Super creepy stuff. I've never known that nodding could be scary. I also especially love it when ghosts mimic the living. +1

item.title: I shouldn't have read this at almost 1 in the morning.
item.content:encoded:
This is spooky. It's the perfect mix of unsettling and genuinely disturbing.

Also, you've been pumping out these high quality articles recently. It's very impressive. And inspiring!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
“My name is Dr. Gradian. I never believed in ghosts until I came face to face with one. So I set out on a quest to capture what I once saw onto video….With no big camera crews following us around, I am joined only by my fellow investigator D-232991 and our equipment tech D-424529. The three of us will travel to the some of the most highly active paranormal locations, where we will spend an entire night, being locked down from dusk until dawn….raw…extreme…these are our Ghost Adventures.”

I don’t think I’ve ever tried doing something spooky, so here’s something I think is a little spooky. Thanks to CadaverCommander, Westrin, PhamtomGuy, CaptainKirby, Alogicab, DarkStuff and PeppersGhost.

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY

Image source is (https://www.flickr.com/photos/26781577@N07/16358789022) under Attribution-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-SA 2.0) (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/)
